document.title: Risk Management Report For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG
authorityOrJurisdiction: Enforcement Regulations of the Medical Device Act

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG digital flat panel X-ray detectors under EN ISO 14971:2012 and related medical electrical equipment standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports the device risk management file by documenting risk analysis, evaluation, control, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production information processes.
keyPoints: The report identifies applicable legal requirements including Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC and medical device regulatory requirements.
The report applies EN 60601-1, EN 60601-1-2, and EN ISO 14971:2012 to risk management activities.
Initial identified risk items include electrical hazards, incorrect measurement, erroneous data transfer, loss or deterioration of function, use error, and information hazards.
After risk controls, nine residual risk items are reported as acceptable and in the Green zone.

Production and post-production information is collected through customer satisfaction surveys, internal audits, internet monitoring, similar product information, legal requirements, clinical evaluation, MFDS information, and press releases.

concepts.evidence: The manufacturer shall use one or more of the following risk control options in the priority order listed: a) Inherent safety by design (Design) b) Protective measures in the medical itself or in manufacturing process (Manufacturing) c) Information for safety (Information)

evidence.claim: Applicable identified energy hazards include line voltage, enclosure leakage current, and patient leakage current.
evidence.evidenceText: R01 Line voltage A ... R02 Enclosure leakage Current A ... R03 Patient leakage current A

evidence.claim: Applicable identified operational hazards include incorrect measurement, erroneous data transfer, loss or deterioration of function, attention failure, rule-based failure, and routine violation.
evidence.evidenceText: R04 Incorrect measurement A ... R05 Erroneous data transfer A ... R06 Loss or deterioration of function A ... R07 Use error Attention failure A ... R08 Rule-based failure A ... R09 Routine Violation A

evidence.claim: Applicable identified information hazards include incomplete instructions for use, inadequate description of performance characteristics, and inadequate specification of accessories to be used with the medical device.
evidence.evidenceText: R10 Labeling Incomplete instructions for use A ... R11 Inadequate description of performance characteristics A ... R12 Operating instructions Inadequate specification of accessories to be used with the medical device A

evidence.claim: The risk acceptability criteria define Green zone 1-6 as acceptable and Red zone 8-25 as not acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Green zone(1~6, Acc, Broadly Acceptable Zone): Risks are acceptable. Red zone(8~25, N/Acc, Not Acceptable Zone)

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as severity multiplied by probability.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk (LEVEL) = Severity x Probability

evidence.claim: Before risk control, R04, R05, and R06 each had severity 4, probability 2, risk 8, and were unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: R04 Incorrect measurement ... 4 2 8 Unacceptable ... R05 Erroneous data transfer ... 4 2 8 Unacceptable ... R06 Loss or deterioration of function ... 4 2 8 Unacceptable

evidence.claim: Risk control measures were implemented through user manual instructions, precautions for use, and methods of usage.
evidence.evidenceText: Describe the precise measurement method in the user manual ... Refer the precautions for use ... Refer the method of usage

evidence.claim: After risk control, R04 through R12 residual risks are listed as acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: R04 ... 4 1 4 Acceptable ... R12 ... 2 1 2 Acceptable

evidence.claim: After risk control, all nine items were controlled as Green zone and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: All of nine (9) items have been controlled as Green zone(Broadly Acceptable Zone) that risks are acceptable.

evidence.claim: A risk-benefit analysis was not related because all residual risks were accepted after risk control.
evidence.evidenceText: The organization has not related to Step 8(Risk Gain Analysis) since residual risks are all accepted after risk control

evidence.claim: No new or different hazards were found after risk control measures.
evidence.evidenceText: New or different types of hazard on the organization’s products have not been found after risk control measure

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks were considered minimized and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Overall residual risks are minimized, therefore, it is considered to be acceptable.

evidence.claim: The risk management report checklist marks risk management activities as meeting EN ISO 14971:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: Are risk management activities meeting the regulations of EN ISO 14971: 2012? ●

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata indicates FDA, but the source text primarily cites CE MDD, Medical Device Act, MFDS, and international requirements; FDA is not present in the source text.
The document contains conflicting hazard counts: it states five hazards are derived, while tables list R04-R12 as nine residual risk items and R01-R12 as twelve hazard IDs.
Initial risk estimation labels R07-R12 as Unacceptable despite risk values of 6 or 4, while the stated criteria define 1-6 as Green zone acceptable.
Several OCR or source-quality issues are present, including misspellings such as Risk(LVEL), Not Acceptable Zoon, and inconsistent wording such as Risk Gain Analysis.
FMEA details are referenced as an attached file ARMR-FMEA-0-001 but are not included in the provided source text.
